original_body:
[ENH]: Add get/set_antialiased to Text objects
### Problem

Currently, Text objects always retrieve their antialiasing state via the global rcParams["text.antialias"], unlike other artists for which this can be configured on a per-artist basis via `set_antialiased` (and read via `set_antialiased`).

### Proposed solution

Add similar getters/setters on Text objects (also adjusting Annotations accordingly, if needed) and use that info in the drawing stage.

Should be relatively easy to implement, except that the slight fiddling needed with backends requires some understanding of backend code (I think we need to replace the access to `rcParams["text.antialiased"]` by going through the GraphicsContext state).
enhanced_title: Add per-artist antialiasing control to Text objects via get/set_antialiased
enhanced_body:
## Summary
Currently, Text objects in matplotlib always use the global `rcParams["text.antialias"]` setting for antialiasing, unlike other artists that support per-artist configuration through `get_antialiased()` and `set_antialiased()`. This enhancement proposes adding similar getter/setter methods to Text objects to enable fine-grained control over text rendering quality.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Create a matplotlib figure with text elements
2. Attempt to set antialiasing on a specific Text object using `set_antialiased()`
3. Observe that the method either doesn't exist or doesn't affect the text rendering
4. Verify that the text still uses the global `rcParams["text.antialias"]` setting

## Expected Behavior
Text objects should:
- Support `get_antialiased()` and `set_antialiased()` methods like other artists
- Allow per-artist antialiasing configuration independent of global settings
- Reflect these settings during rendering
- Maintain backward compatibility with existing code using global rcParams

## Actual Behavior
Text objects:
- Only respect the global `rcParams["text.antialias"]` setting
- Lack `get_antialiased()` and `set_antialiased()` methods
- Cannot be configured independently from other text elements
- Require changing global settings to affect all text rendering

## Scope / Affected Areas
Key components that need modification:
- Text artist class implementation
- Annotation class (which inherits from Text)
- Backend rendering code that accesses `rcParams["text.antialias"]`
- GraphicsContext state handling for text rendering

Affected files likely include:
- `lib/matplotlib/text.py` (Text class implementation)
- `lib/matplotlib/artist.py` (base artist methods)
- Backend-specific rendering code
- Any code that currently directly accesses `rcParams["text.antialias"]`

The implementation will require:
- Adding property methods to Text class
- Modifying backend rendering to use the artist's antialiasing state
- Ensuring proper inheritance for Annotation objects
- Maintaining compatibility with existing code
